# Kyokara

> Kyokara is an AI-first programming language and runtime. It is designed for machine generation, machine verification, and machine refactoring â€” with humans as reviewers and operators. The compiler is an API that emits structured JSON (default diagnostics/hole specs/symbol graph, optional typed AST, fix patches). The runtime enforces capability-based sandboxing and deterministic replay. Written in Rust.

## Core Concepts

- [Design Specification](https://github.com/kyokaralang/kyokara/blob/main/docs/design-v0.md): Full language and runtime design document covering syntax, type system, effects, contracts, IR, toolchain, and runtime.
- [README](https://github.com/kyokaralang/kyokara/blob/main/README.md): Project overview, motivation, language examples, architecture, and roadmap.

## Language Features

- Convention-based modules: file path = module path (`math.ky` â†’ module `math`), `import math` binds the namespace, `from math import gcd` binds members directly, no source-level `module Path` declaration
- Private by default: use `pub` keyword to export functions, types, and capabilities across modules
- Pure by default: functions without `with` clauses cannot perform side effects
- Explicit capabilities: `with net`, `with db`, `with clock`, `with secrets` â€” deny-by-default, manifest-enforced
- ADTs and exhaustive pattern matching: `type Result<T, E> = Ok(T) | Err(E)`
- Contracts: `contract` sections with `requires`/`ensures`/`invariant` clauses and `old()` â€” checked at runtime, optionally verified by SMT (restricted fragment, best-effort)
- Property-based tests: `property name(x: T <- Gen.auto()) where (pred) { expr }` as first-class syntax, `<-` generator bindings (`Gen.auto()`, `Gen.int()`, `Gen.int_range(min, max)`, etc.), optional `where` constraints (discard-based), bodies type-checked (must return Bool)
- Typed holes: `_` compiles to structured hole specs with expected type, effects, and constraints
- Pipeline operator: `x |> f(a: 1)` desugars to `f(x, a: 1)`
- Error propagation: `expr?` desugars to early-return match on `Result`
- Native integer bitwise operators: `&`, `|`, `^`, `~`, `<<`, `>>` on `Int`; `>>` is arithmetic right shift; shift counts must be in `0..63`
- Local bindings: `let` is immutable by default; `var` introduces a mutable local slot; assignment is `x = expr` to an existing mutable local identifier; in v1, lambdas cannot capture mutable locals
- Loop control surface: `while (cond) { ... }`, `for (pattern in source) { ... }`, `break`, `continue`; `for` patterns support destructuring but must be irrefutable, and `for` sources are any traversables (ranges, collections, producer chains)
- No null, no exceptions: `Option<T>` and `Result<T, E>` only (builtin types, available without imports)

## Compiler Architecture

- [stdx](https://github.com/kyokaralang/kyokara/tree/main/crates/stdx): Shared utilities (leaf crate, no kyokara deps)
- [span](https://github.com/kyokaralang/kyokara/tree/main/crates/span): FileId, Span, TextRange â€” source locations
- [intern](https://github.com/kyokaralang/kyokara/tree/main/crates/intern): String interning via lasso
- [diagnostics](https://github.com/kyokaralang/kyokara/tree/main/crates/diagnostics): Diagnostic, Severity, Fix types
- [parser](https://github.com/kyokaralang/kyokara/tree/main/crates/parser): Tree-agnostic recursive-descent parser emitting SyntaxKind + Event stream
- [syntax](https://github.com/kyokaralang/kyokara/tree/main/crates/syntax): Lossless CST via rowan + logos lexer + typed AST wrappers
- [hir-def](https://github.com/kyokaralang/kyokara/tree/main/crates/hir-def): HIR data types (items, bodies, exprs, patterns, types, effects, holes) + CST-to-HIR lowering
- [hir-ty](https://github.com/kyokaralang/kyokara/tree/main/crates/hir-ty): Type inference, unification, exhaustiveness checking, effect checking
- [hir](https://github.com/kyokaralang/kyokara/tree/main/crates/hir): High-level facade for semantic queries
- [kir](https://github.com/kyokaralang/kyokara/tree/main/crates/kir): SSA-based intermediate representation â€” block parameters (not phi nodes), reuses hir-ty::Ty, arena-based values/blocks/functions. Inst enum includes Const, Binary, Unary, RecordCreate, FieldGet, RecordUpdate, AdtConstruct, Call, Assert, Hole, BlockParam, FnParam, AdtFieldGet, FnRef. Text format printer, builder API. Well-formedness validator enforces Bool conditions, Fn call targets, Record/Adt field bases, predecessor edges, duplicate cases. HIRâ†’KIR lowering pass with ADT switch dedup/catch-all/nested-literal checks, sequential match early termination, contract old() pre-state, function references
- [codegen](https://github.com/kyokaralang/kyokara/tree/main/crates/codegen): WASM code generation from KIR. Compiles KIR modules to WASM binary via wasm-encoder. Value representation: Intâ†’i64, Floatâ†’f64, Bool/Unitâ†’i32, ADT/Recordâ†’i32 (linear memory pointers). Bump allocator, ADT tag+fields layout, record sorted-by-name fields. Structured control flow: if/else for branches, block/br_table for switch. Supports scalar arithmetic, comparisons, let bindings, function calls, ADT construction/matching, record creation/field access, contract assertions. Deferred: closures, strings, lists, maps, intrinsics, capabilities
- [eval](https://github.com/kyokaralang/kyokara/tree/main/crates/eval): Tree-walking interpreter with scoped environments, core stdlib via canonical API surface (collection-first traversal on `List`/`MutableList`/`Deque`/producer values with transforms including `scan` and `flat_map`, and terminals including `count()/count(predicate)/contains(value)/frequencies()` and `any/all/find`; traversal constructors are `start..<end` and `seed.unfold(step)`; constrained call families are generic across builtin and user-defined APIs but resolution is limited to arity and/or declared named-argument presence, never types; `List` supports persistent `set/update`; collection constructors live under `collections.*`: `collections.List.new()`, `collections.Map.new()`, `collections.Set.new()`, `collections.BitSet.new(size)`, `collections.MutableList.new()`, `collections.MutableList.from_list()`, `collections.MutableMap.new()`, `collections.MutableMap.with_capacity(capacity)`, `collections.MutableSet.new()`, `collections.MutableSet.with_capacity(capacity)`, `collections.MutablePriorityQueue.new_min()/new_max()`, `collections.MutableBitSet.new(size)`, `collections.Deque.new()`; `MutableList` also exposes stack-style `push()/insert()/last()/pop()/extend(List<T>)`, dense index helpers `get/set/update`, and indexed deletion via `delete_at` (chainable) or `remove_at` (returns the removed element); `MutableMap` exposes `insert/get/get_or_insert_with/contains/remove/len/keys/values/is_empty`, with `get_or_insert_with(key, fn() => value)` as the canonical compute-if-absent helper for memoization and caching; `MutablePriorityQueue` provides alias-visible prioritized worklists with `push/peek/pop/len/is_empty`, explicit min/max construction, and stable insertion-order ties for equal priorities; `Map`/`Set` and their mutable variants are trait-backed and accept user `Hash + Eq` keys/elements; for nominal keys/elements, derive or implement those traits explicitly (for example `type Point derive(Eq, Hash) = { x: Int, y: Int }`). `with_capacity(capacity)` is a minimum live-element hint only, and primitive mutable keys/elements (`Int`, `String`, `Char`, `Bool`, `Unit`) use an internal ordered open-address fast path rather than a separate public specialized map/set type. `BitSet`/`MutableBitSet` expose `test/set/reset/flip`, set algebra, `count/size/is_empty`, and ascending `values()`; `flat_map` accepts callback results from `Seq`, `List`, `MutableList`, `Deque`, and nominal user types implementing `IntoTraversal<T>`; pure digest helpers live under `hash.*`, with `import hash` and `hash.md5(text)` as the canonical MD5 surface, while `String.md5()` remains a convenience alias over the same lowercase hexadecimal digest; `String` is Unicode-scalar based for `len`/indexing/`substring`/`chars`, exposes constrained-family `starts_with(prefix)` / `starts_with(prefix, start: idx)`, and `Char` exposes `to_string()/code()/is_decimal_digit()/to_decimal_digit()/to_digit(radix)` with ASCII digit/letter conversion semantics and `radix` constrained to `2..=36`; `Int` also has native bitwise operators `&`, `|`, `^`, `~`, `<<`, `>>` plus methods `abs/pow/to_string/to_float`), builtin Option/Result types plus builtin collection types (`List`, `MutableList`, `MutableMap`, `MutableSet`, `MutablePriorityQueue`, `Deque`, `Map`, `Set`, `BitSet`, `MutableBitSet`), identity-based core dispatch (shadow-safe), runtime contract enforcement (requires/ensures/old), capability manifest enforcement (`--caps` flag, deny-by-default when manifest present), and type-owned constructors: use `Type.Variant(...)` / `Type.Variant` directly or import variants explicitly with `from Type import Variant`.
- [fmt](https://github.com/kyokaralang/kyokara/tree/main/crates/fmt): Canonical code formatter â€” Wadler-Lindig Doc IR, 2-space indent, 100-col width, import sorting, comment preservation, verbatim error fallback
- [refactor](https://github.com/kyokaralang/kyokara/tree/main/crates/refactor): Semantic refactor engine â€” CST-based rename (function, type, capability, variant), add missing match cases, add missing capability annotation, transactional verification (apply edits in-memory, re-check, return Verified/Failed/Skipped status)
- [lsp](https://github.com/kyokaralang/kyokara/tree/main/crates/lsp): LSP server with salsa incrementality â€” diagnostics, hover, go-to-definition, find references, completion, code actions (quickfixes), formatting
- [pbt](https://github.com/kyokaralang/kyokara/tree/main/crates/pbt): Property-based testing â€” choice-sequence engine (SplitMix64 RNG, record/replay), type-driven generators (scalars, ADTs, records, Option, Result, List, Map), 4-pass shrinker (zero-suffix, block deletion, binary-search minimize, pair swap), corpus persistence, contract-for-free testing of `requires`/`ensures`/`invariant` functions, explicit `property` declarations (bodies type-checked, must return Bool), refinement-constrained generation (rejection sampling)
- [api](https://github.com/kyokaralang/kyokara/tree/main/crates/api): JSON serialization of compiler outputs (diagnostics, holes, symbol graph, optional typed AST via check options, patches, refactor results)
- [cli](https://github.com/kyokaralang/kyokara/tree/main/crates/cli): `kyokara check` (`--emit typed-ast` requires `--format json`), `kyokara run`, `kyokara fmt`, `kyokara refactor`, `kyokara test`, `kyokara lsp`

## Optional
